name: Moià, Sant Llorenç Savall, and Sant Miquel del Fai Loop
name_original: Sant Miguel de Fai desde Sant Martí de Centelles — vuelta al Parque Natural del Montseny
description: This difficult 67.1-mile road cycling route from Sant Martí de Centelles explores the scenic Montseny Natural Park and Sant Miquel del Fai.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sant Martí De Centelles traverse a geographically varied landscape, encompassing the Congost valley, the Cingles de Bertí cliffs, and the high plateaus of Pla de la Garga and Costes de Collsuspina. The region offers diverse terrain, from riverine forests in the lower valleys to challenging ascents through impressive natural barriers. Cyclists encounter significant elevation changes, with routes leading to the highest points like Puigsagordi (984m) and Agullola de Sant Martí (875m). This varied topography provides a range of road cycling challenges and scenic viewpoints. Best road cycling routes around Sant Martí De Centelles The most popular road cycling route is Moià, Sant Llorenç Savall, and Sant Miquel del Fai Loop, a 67.1 miles (107.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic Montseny Natural Park and the area around Sant Miquel del Fai. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Centelles, a difficult 43.5 miles (70.0 km) path. This route features challenging ascents to higher elevations, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Taradell Monument – Saint Vincent of Malla Church loop from Centelles, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past cultural landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Martí De Centelles is defined by the Congost valley, the Cingles de Bertí, and high plateaus, offering challenging climbs and rewarding descents. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Sant Martí De Centelles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Martí De Centelles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: Sant Martí de Centelles offers a geographically varied landscape, perfect for road cycling. You'll encounter the lower Congost valley with its riverine forests, impressive natural cliffs of the Cingles de Bertí, and higher plateaus like Pla de la Garga and Costes de Collsuspina. This means a mix of challenging ascents, rewarding descents, and scenic flat sections, providing diverse cycling experiences.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging climbs. The Cingles de Bertí feature impressive vertical cliffs with several passes offering significant elevation gains. You can also tackle the highest elevations in the area, such as Puigsagordi (984m) and Agullola de Sant Martí (875m). A particularly demanding climb near Tagamanent village features an average gradient of 9.1% with sections reaching 15-20%.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Sant Martí de Centelles offer breathtaking views. You can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Congost valley, the Cingles de Bertí, and the distant peaks of the Pyrenees from higher elevations. Notable natural formations include the Castellets and La Trona in the Valldaneu valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26752015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Pullosa (917 m) – Collsuspina loop from Centelles&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, the area is rich in historical heritage. You can cycle past sites like the medieval Centelles Castle, located at 855 meters on the Agullola de Sant Martí, or visit churches such as Sant Martí de Centelles and Sant Pere de Valldaneu. The 18th-century Pont de l'Abella is another historical point of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taradell Monument – Saint Vincent of Malla Church loop from Centelles&lt;/a&gt; leads through varied terrain and past cultural landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the diverse ecosystems and varied terrain suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter might bring cooler temperatures to the higher elevations. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes?
A: Yes, despite the challenging terrain, Sant Martí de Centelles offers options for all ability levels. There are 8 easy road cycling routes available, providing gentler gradients and shorter distances suitable for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed ride. These routes often explore the lower valley areas.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Sant Martí de Centelles are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983225693/moia-sant-llorenc-savall-and-sant-miquel-del-fai-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moià, Sant Llorenç Savall, and Sant Miquel del Fai Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Formic Pass (1,145 m) – El Brull loop from Sant Martí de Centelles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: The road cycling routes in Sant Martí de Centelles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any specific attractions like waterfalls or caves along the cycling routes?
A: While primarily known for its cycling terrain, the region does have natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight that can be incorporated into longer rides, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983225693/moia-sant-llorenc-savall-and-sant-miquel-del-fai-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moià, Sant Llorenç Savall, and Sant Miquel del Fai Loop&lt;/a&gt;. There are also several caves in the wider area, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balma la Miquelona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the road cycling routes in Sant Martí de Centelles typically?
A: The routes vary significantly in length to suit different preferences and fitness levels. You can find shorter routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31248257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major de Vic – View of Montseny from Malla loop from Centelles&lt;/a&gt; at 38.3 km, to much longer and more demanding ones like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983225693/moia-sant-llorenc-savall-and-sant-miquel-del-fai-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moià, Sant Llorenç Savall, and Sant Miquel del Fai Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 107.9 km.

Q: Are there bike-friendly cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: While specific bike-friendly cafes are not detailed in the route data, the region's popularity for outdoor activities suggests that towns and villages along the routes, such as Centelles, will offer places to stop for refreshments. The Tourist Information Point at l'Oller can also provide guidance on local amenities.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists with bikes in Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: The provided information does not detail specific public transport options for cyclists with bikes in Sant Martí de Centelles. It's advisable to check with local transport providers or the Tourist Information Point at l'Oller for current information regarding bike carriage on public transport.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in Catalonia or Sant Martí de Centelles?
A: There is no indication of specific permits required for general road cycling in Sant Martí de Centelles or Catalonia within the provided information. However, always respect local regulations, especially when cycling through natural parks or protected areas, and stay on designated roads.
tips:
The Church of Sant Jaume de Viladrover is a Romanesque work in Brull (Osona) protected as a Cultural Property of Local Interest.

   Sant Jaume de Viladrover or dels Bastons is isolated, near a farmhouse, 2 km from Balenyà. It is a one-nave building, covered with a barrel vault with lunettes and toral arches, topped by a semicircular apse decorated with arches under a sawtooth frieze built at the beginning of the 20th century. On the north side, the body of the sacristy was added. It preserves the original door in the south wall, with a semi-circular arch with two-tone shutters, all of which have been blinded. The current access is on the west facade where there is a rectangular door under a 17th century semicircular pediment and a cross-shaped window. The most remarkable thing is the slightly rhomboid bell tower, three stories high, which rests on the vault at the west end of the nave. Despite the modifications from the Baroque era, it preserves some of the twined Romanesque windows with column and crescent capital and the decoration of arches under the Lombard-type saw-tooth frieze.

Moià is the most important town on the Moianès plateau. Added to its natural environment, with its forests, paths, farmhouses and rural houses. Places of interest include the Toll caves and Toixoneres caves, the Rafael Casanova House-Museum, the municipal park, the Nou mill, the house of the tenor Francesc Viñas and the Clarà castle.

Sant Llorenç Savall is a municipality in the region of Vallés Occidental, province of Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.

   It is located in the northern part of its region, on the border with those of Bages el Moyanés and Vallés Oriental. Among its historical-artistic heritage, the ruins of the castle of La Pera and the Romanesque church of Sant Feliu de Vallcarca stand out, as well as the remains of a prehistoric dolmen.
This rock looks like Indiana Jones, but bigger still!
You can stop for a coffee in St Feliu de Codines before starting the route to the gorge.

The Monastery of Sant Miquel del Fai is one of the most surprising must-sees for those who pass through Barcelona. It is located just 50 kilometers from the Catalan capital and is one of those places that you fall in love with. The church, on top of a mountain and suspended over the cliff (it seems magic), leaves one of the most special visions of the Catalan mountain range. A hidden treasure known to few, located in the middle of a beautiful natural environment near the town of Bigues i Riells.

When you enter the natural area, everything captures the senses. The Monastery of Sant Miquel del Fai almost hangs from the cliff of the mountain at the top, presiding over a space where rock and water roam freely. The route to the Monastery of Sant Miquel del Fai is a spectacular walk, which can be done on foot or by car to reach the historic site. The visit to the church is paid, as the site has owners and is private property. But paying for them is worth it.
The road is a little broken up and rocky for about 100 meters. After that it turns into a narrow road which is perfect for cycling.

faqs.question: How difficult is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and is a challenging road ride. It covers approximately 108 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of around 1,840 meters, demanding a high level of fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride. You can expect long climbs, especially in the Montseny Natural Park area, with varied surfaces. The route near Sant Miquel del Fai offers fantastic views with tall cliffs.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montseny Natural Park area experiences diverse climates. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while higher peaks might experience colder conditions in winter. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass several notable spots, including the Sant Jaume de Viladrover Church, the charming Moià Old Town, and the village of Sant Llorenç Savall. A significant highlight is Sant Miquel del Fai, an 11th-century Benedictine monastery and historical site, offering impressive views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Sant Martí de Centelles. You should be able to find parking options within the town, though specific cyclist parking facilities are not detailed. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Can I reach the starting point by public transport?
faqs.answer: Sant Martí de Centelles is the starting point. While public transport options might exist to reach the town, direct services specifically for cyclists with bikes may be limited. Checking local bus or train schedules to Sant Martí de Centelles is recommended.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits are generally required for riding on public roads within the Montseny Natural Park. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Sant Miquel del Fai complex, there might be entrance fees for the site itself.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: As this is a demanding road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for dogs. The long distances, elevation changes, and potential for traffic make it unsafe and impractical for most dogs to accompany cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will pass through several settlements like Moià Old Town and Sant Llorenç Savall, as well as the starting point Sant Martí de Centelles, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to resupply with food and water.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the diverse and beautiful landscapes of the Montseny Natural Park, a Biosphere Reserve. This includes Mediterranean flora, holm oak and cork oak forests at lower elevations, transitioning to oak groves and beech forests higher up. The region is rich in water, with streams and waterfalls, and offers stunning views, especially near Sant Miquel del Fai.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails, including the Camí Ral de Manresa a Vic, El Camí IT7TR1 El Camí de Llevant: de Montserrat a Salses, and Via Ausetana.
